  ResearchArea: Carbon capture storage and utilization (CCSU), Process Intensification, Environmental Engineering, Biochemical and Bioprocess Engineering, Waste to wealth, Nanomaterial synthesis and characterization, Photochemistry and Photocatalysis, Plasmonics, X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy, Heterogeneous catalysis (tri-phasic reactions)

Summary: The Community 1 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Dr. Gaurav Ashok Bhaduri, focusing on a diverse range of research areas including carbon capture storage and utilization, process intensification, and nanomaterial synthesis and characterization. Their work also encompasses environmental engineering, biochemical and bioprocess engineering, and waste-to-wealth conversion. This community is characterized by its interdisciplinary approach to addressing complex problems through the application of advanced technologies such as photocatalysis and plasmonics.

members: bsbe:The Receptor Biology laboratory at IIT Jammu focuses on understanding the molecular mechanisms of G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs) in physiological and pathophysiological processes. GPCRs play a vital role as mediators of cellular signaling and are the largest receptor superfamily encoded by the human genome. Recognizing their involvement in numerous biological functions and their significance as drug targets
bsbe:the lab aims to unravel the intricate workings of GPCRs at the molecular level to develop safer and more effective therapeutics
bsbe:the lab employs a range of research techniques
bsbe:and pharmacological approaches. By utilizing multiple methods
bsbe:we strive to comprehensively understand GPCR function and its molecular interactions and signaling pathways
bsbe:The lab's research themes focus on two key areas. Firstly

reports.text:
Community 3 in IIT Jammu EE Department:
  Faculty: Mithu Baidya
  PhDStudent: Abdul Rouf War, Madhurjya Protim Borah, Neeraj, Sarfraz Ahmed
  ResearchArea: The Receptor Biology laboratory at IIT Jammu focuses on understanding the molecular mechanisms of G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs) in physiological and pathophysiological processes. GPCRs play a vital role as mediators of cellular signaling and are the largest receptor superfamily encoded by the human genome. Recognizing their involvement in numerous biological functions and their significance as drug targets, the lab aims to unravel the intricate workings of GPCRs at the molecular level to develop safer and more effective therapeutics, To achieve this, the lab employs a range of research techniques, including biochemical, biophysical, chemogenetic, and pharmacological approaches. By utilizing multiple methods, we strive to comprehensively understand GPCR function and its molecular interactions and signaling pathways, The lab's research themes focus on two key areas. Firstly, Molecular Pharmacology, Drug Discovery, Structure based drug designing, Receptor cell biology
  Relationships: RESEARCHES_IN: 14, SUPERVISED_BY: 4, STUDIES: 4

Summary: The Community 3 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Faculty member Mithu Baidya. The group consists of PhD students Abdul Rouf War, Madhurjya Protim Borah, Neeraj, and Sarfraz Ahmed. They focus on understanding the molecular mechanisms of G protein-coupled receptors (GPCRs) in physiological and pathophysiological processes through biochemical, biophysical, chemogenetic, and pharmacological approaches.
reports.summary: The Community 3 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Faculty member Mithu Baidya. The group consists of PhD students Abdul Rouf War, Madhurjya Protim Borah, Neeraj, and Sarfraz Ahmed.   ResearchArea: Computational neuroscience, neurophysiology and neural oscillations, biomedical signal processing (EEG/MEG), and neurological disorders and rehabilitation, Brain computer Interface

Summary: The Community 4 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Faculty member Nitesh Singh Malan. This group focuses on interdisciplinary research in computational neuroscience, neurophysiology, neural oscillations, biomedical signal processing (EEG/MEG), and neurological disorders and rehabilitation, with a particular emphasis on Brain-Computer Interface applications. The faculty member has 5 related research projects or publications in these areas, indicating an active and specialized research community.

members: bsbe:Our lab at IIT Jammu focuses on understanding the complexities of female reproductive tissues
bsbe:particularly in the context of gynecological diseases and cancers. This research emphasizes the significance of both coding and noncoding regulatory elements in the genome (REGULOME)
bsbe:which drive cellular heterogeneity and plasticity. By employing advanced Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) techniques and computational models

bsbe:we aim to map these regulatory elements to uncover mechanisms underlying disease progression and treatment resistance. The ultimate goal is to develop targeted therapies that address tissue heterogeneity and improve women's reproductive health
bsbe:Genomic and epigenomic-based tissue regulation in development and disease
bsbe:Multi-omics and big data intergration to investigate tissue heterogeneity and cell plasticity
bsbe:Developing AI/ML models utilizing NGS-based genomic data

reports.text:
Community 5 in IIT Jammu EE Department:
  Faculty: Santhilal Subhash
  PhDStudent: Isha Mukherjee, Shalini Gupta, Uma Mahesh
  ResearchArea: Our lab at IIT Jammu focuses on understanding the complexities of female reproductive tissues, particularly in the context of gynecological diseases and cancers. This research emphasizes the significance of both coding and noncoding regulatory elements in the genome (REGULOME), which drive cellular heterogeneity and plasticity. By employing advanced Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) techniques and computational models, we aim to map these regulatory elements to uncover mechanisms underlying disease progression and treatment resistance. The ultimate goal is to develop targeted therapies that address tissue heterogeneity and improve women's reproductive health, Key Focus Areas, Reproductive Biology, Genomics & Single Cell Biology, Multi-omics, Bioinformatics, Genomic and epigenomic-based tissue regulation in development and disease, Multi-omics and big data intergration to investigate tissue heterogeneity and cell plasticity, Developing AI/ML models utilizing NGS-based genomic data
  Relationships: RESEARCHES_IN: 12, SUPERVISED_BY: 3, STUDIES: 3

Summary: The Community 5 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Faculty member Santhilal Subhash. The group consists of PhD students Isha Mukherjee, Shalini Gupta, and Uma Mahesh who are supervised by Dr. Subhash. Their research focuses on understanding female reproductive tissues, particularly in the context of gynecological diseases and cancers, using advanced Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) techniques and computational models to develop targeted therapies.
reports.summary: The Community 5 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by Faculty member Santhilal Subhash. The group consists of PhD students Isha Mukherjee, Shalini Gupta, and Uma Mahesh who are supervised by Dr. Subhash. Their research focuses on understanding female reproductive tissues, particularly in the context of gynecological diseases and cancers, using advanced Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) techniques and computational models to develop targeted therapies.

reports.text:
Community 6 in IIT Jammu EE Department:
  Faculty: Shirsendu Ghosh
  PhDStudent: Ananya Anand, Vanshika
  ResearchArea: Development of Super/high resolution optical microscopy and Single Molecule Spectroscopy technique, Membrane Biophysics, Immunological signaling pathways, Allosteric Signaling in Proteins, Super-resolution microscopy, Immunology, Membrane Biophysics, Allosteric Signaling of Protein
  Relationships: RESEARCHES_IN: 5, SUPERVISED_BY: 2, STUDIES: 2

Summary: The Community 6 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by faculty member Shirsendu Ghosh. This group focuses on developing advanced optical microscopy techniques, including super-resolution microscopy, as well as studying membrane biophysics and immunological signaling pathways. They also explore allosteric signaling in proteins. The community includes PhD students Ananya Anand and Vanshika, who are supervised by Shirsendu Ghosh.

reports.text:
Community 8 in IIT Jammu EE Department:
  Department: Department of Biosciences and Bioengineering
  Faculty: Ajeet Kumar Sharma, Jigmi Basumatary
  ResearchArea: Biophysics, Systems Biology and Physical Bioinformatics, Thermodynamics and Statistical Physics, Non-equilibrium statistical mechanics and Biophysics, include key technologies such as single-molecule localization microscopy (SMLM), light-sheet microscopy, optical tweezers, and photoacoustic/ultrasound imaging. He adopts a multidisciplinary approach that integrates optical engineering with other science and engineering disciplines, along with rigorous qualitative and quantitative analysis. The overarching goal of his research is to develop and extend imaging techniques to address fundamental biological questions
  Relationships: RESEARCHES_IN: 9, MEMBER_OF: 2

Summary: Community 8 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by faculty members Ajeet Kumar Sharma and Jigmi Basumatary. They conduct interdisciplinary research in Biophysics, Systems Biology, and Physical Bioinformatics, focusing on developing imaging techniques using technologies such as single-molecule localization microscopy (SMLM) and optical tweezers. Their work integrates optical engineering with other science and engineering disciplines to address fundamental biological questions.
reports.summary: Community 8 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by faculty members Ajeet Kumar Sharma and Jigmi Basumatary. Their work integrates optical engineering with other science and engineering disciplines to address fundamental biological questions.

reports.text:
Community 10 in IIT Jammu EE Department:
  Faculty: Shirsha Bose
  PhDStudent: Amir Ashraf, Prangya, Sathish
  ResearchArea: Experimental and Computational Biomechanics, fabricating novel biomaterials, analyzing the mechanics of biological materials and bioinspired materials, and understanding the interaction of medical devices with neighbouring tissues, Biomaterials, Blood Clot Mechanics, Mechanics of calcified collagen fibre
  Relationships: RESEARCHES_IN: 7, SUPERVISED_BY: 3, STUDIES: 3

Summary: The Community 10 in IIT Jammu's Electrical Engineering Department is led by faculty member Shirsha Bose. The group consists of PhD students Amir Ashraf, Prangya, and Sathish, who work together on research areas including experimental and computational biomechanics, biomaterials, and blood clot mechanics. Their research focuses on understanding the mechanics of biological materials, fabricating novel biomaterials, and analyzing bioinspired materials, with a specific interest in calcified collagen fibers.
